rows.design_decision: Adopting the three-layer angular signal decomposition model I(u,v) = DC + a·u + b·v + ε(u,v) instead of frequency-domain methods (e.g., 2D-DFT).
rows.motivation: Low angular resolution (e.g., 9x9) makes frequency-domain features too coarse to effectively decouple material reflectance from geometric disparity.
rows.sota_gap: Overcomes the failure of traditional frequency-domain methods at low angular resolutions by using spatial-domain geometric features for high-precision physical decoupling.
rows.scenario: Light field data with discrete low angular resolution (9x9), especially mixed scenes containing non-Lambertian surfaces (specular/scattering).
rows.evidence: RF classifier combining 5 geometric features achieves 89.6% accuracy and 0.962 AUC; angular_gradient feature has a Cohen's d effect size of 0.983.

rows.design_decision: Utilizing geometric features (angular gradient, angular correlation) with Random Forest for explicit Lambertian/Non-Lambertian binary classification.
rows.motivation: Non-Lambertian surfaces destroy EPI line structures, requiring explicit material property extraction to guide depth estimation, which implicit learning fails to provide.
rows.sota_gap: Surpasses implicit feature learning or frequency-based methods by providing physically interpretable features that maintain high classification accuracy under 9x9 resolution limits.
rows.scenario: UrbanLF-Syn mixed scenes and Non-Lambertian datasets containing complex materials like highlights and scattering.
rows.evidence: Phase 1 empirical evidence shows RF classifier reaches 89.6% accuracy and 0.962 AUC, validating geometric feature efficacy.

rows.design_decision: Designing the GeometricDualMask dual-branch architecture to adaptively route features to Lambertian EPI and Non-Lambertian geometric branches via masks.
rows.motivation: Traditional single-EPI networks suffer severe performance degradation in mixed-material scenes because non-Lambertian surfaces violate the angular cone geometric constraint.
rows.sota_gap: Prevents the performance drop of single-EPI networks in mixed scenes by explicitly routing features to specialized branches, preserving EPI slope extraction for Lambertian regions.
rows.scenario: Complex urban/mixed scenes (170 scenes in UrbanLF-Syn) containing both Lambertian and non-Lambertian regions.
rows.evidence: Mixed (Urban) MAE reaches 0.081 (well below the 0.22 target threshold), and mask gap is 0.041 (better than the 0.08 target).

rows.design_decision: Implementing domain-balanced sampling and WeightedRandomSampler for joint training across heterogeneous datasets.
rows.motivation: Severe data imbalance between Lambertian scenes (HCInew) and Non-Lambertian/Mixed scenes (only 4 training scenes for Non-Lambertian) causes the model to bias toward the majority domain.
rows.sota_gap: Solves the low depth estimation accuracy in the non-Lambertian domain caused by long-tail data distribution in traditional joint training, maintaining multi-domain exposure balance.
rows.scenario: Cross-domain joint training, specifically when Non-Lambertian training data is extremely scarce (only 4 scenes).
rows.evidence: Overall MAE reaches 0.133 (below the 0.20 target), proving the model maintains global generalization under extreme data imbalance.

rows.design_decision: Conducting root cause analysis of EPI failure through 132 experiments across 16 research directions to define physical boundaries.
rows.motivation: Existing literature blindly applies attention mechanisms or loss variants to fix EPI without understanding the physical root cause (violation of angular cone constraints) of EPI failure.
rows.sota_gap: Fills the theoretical gap regarding EPI physical boundaries in light field depth estimation, preventing computational waste on ineffective directions like merely increasing network complexity.
rows.scenario: All light field depth estimation methods relying on EPI line structures, specifically analyzing failures in specular, scattering, and complex-texture Lambertian scenes.
rows.evidence: Documentation of 132 autonomous experiments evaluating 16 directions (dual-branch, attention, defocus heads, etc.), establishing the absolute performance ceiling of EPI methods.

rows.design_decision: Generating pixel-level domain classification masks and fusing depth predictions via mask-weighted blending after channel-balanced projection.
rows.motivation: Simple feature concatenation or hard switching causes depth discontinuities and artifacts at material boundaries, requiring pixel-level soft fusion for smooth depth transitions.
rows.sota_gap: Outperforms traditional hard-threshold segmentation or post-heuristic fusion methods by enabling end-to-end mask learning and seamless depth map fusion.
rows.scenario: Boundary regions between Lambertian and non-Lambertian materials (e.g., highlight edges, reflective object borders) requiring high-precision depth transitions.
rows.evidence: GeometricDualMask model achieves a mask gap of 0.041 (better than the 0.08 target), proving effective mask learning and smooth fusion boundaries.
